skill.name: python-executor
skill.description: Execute Python code in a safe sandboxed environment via [inference.sh](https://inference.sh). Pre-installed: NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, requests, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, Playwright, MoviePy, Pillow, OpenCV, trimesh, and 100+ more libraries. Use for: data processing, web scraping, image manipulation, video creation, 3D model processing, PDF generation, API calls, automation scripts. Triggers: python, execute code, run script, web scraping, data analysis, image processing, video editing, 3D models, automation, pandas, matplotlib
skill.summary: Execute Python code in a safe sandboxed environment via inference.sh with 100+ pre-installed libraries for data processing, web scraping, image/video manipulation, and automation.
skill.author: skillssh

security_audit.summary: All 69 static findings are false positives from markdown code blocks and documentation URLs. The skill contains only legitimate documentation for a Python execution service with no executable code, security vulnerabilities, or malicious intent. Verified as safe for publication.

content.user_title: Execute Python Code with 100+ Libraries in a Safe Sandbox
content.value_statement: Run Python code securely in a sandboxed environment without local installation. Access 100+ pre-installed libraries including Pandas, NumPy, BeautifulSoup, MoviePy, and OpenCV for data processing, web scraping, image manipulation, video creation, and automation tasks.
actual_capabilities: Execute Python code in a safe sandboxed environment with 8GB or 16GB RAM
Process and analyze data using NumPy, Pandas, and SciPy
Scrape websites with requests, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, or Playwright
Create and edit images with Pillow and OpenCV
Generate videos with MoviePy and FFmpeg
Process 3D models using trimesh and open3d
limitations: CPU-only execution without GPU access for ML workloads
Non-interactive environment requires file output instead of display
Requires inference.sh CLI installation and authentication

use_cases.title: Data Analysis and Visualization
use_cases.description: Process large datasets with Pandas, create visualizations with Matplotlib, and export results as CSV or PNG files
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts working with structured data

use_cases.title: Web Scraping Automation
use_cases.description: Extract data from websites using HTTP clients and HTML parsers, or automate browsers with Selenium for dynamic content

use_cases.title: Media Processing Pipeline
use_cases.description: Manipulate images, create videos with text overlays, or process 3D models entirely in Python without local dependencies

prompt_templates.title: Basic Python Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute this Python code and return the output: {code}

prompt_templates.title: Data Analysis with Pandas
prompt_templates.prompt: Run Python code to analyze this data: {data}. Use Pandas for processing and save visualizations to outputs/
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing structured datasets with statistical analysis

prompt_templates.title: Web Scraping Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Write and execute Python code to scrape data from {url}. Extract {target_data} and save the results to outputs/data.json

prompt_templates.title: Media Processing Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute Python to process media files. Use {libraries} to manipulate the {media_type} and save results to outputs/
prompt_templates.scenario: Image, video, or 3D model processing tasks

output_examples.input: Execute Python code to calculate fibonacci sequence
output: Running Python execution with 8GB RAM allocation
Files available for download: products.csv (4.2 KB)

output_examples.input: Create a bar chart from this data
output: Created bar chart showing sales by region
Files available for download: sales_chart.png (156 KB)

best_practices: Always save outputs to the outputs/ directory for automatic file retrieval after execution
Set appropriate timeout values based on task complexity (30s default, up to 300s maximum)
Use high_memory variant (16GB RAM) when working with large datasets or complex media processing
Write non-interactive code by using save methods instead of display functions like plt.show()
anti_patterns: Do not use interactive commands like plt.show() or input() as they will cause execution to hang
Avoid installing new packages with pip as only pre-installed libraries are available
Do not attempt GPU or ML model training as the environment is CPU-only
Never hardcode sensitive credentials in code as the execution environment is not persistent

faq.question: Is my Python code executed safely?
faq.answer: Yes, code runs in an isolated sandboxed subprocess with no access to your local system or files.

faq.question: Can I install additional Python packages?
faq.answer: No, only the 100+ pre-installed libraries are available. Common packages like Pandas, NumPy, requests, and OpenCV are included.

faq.question: How do I get files created during execution?
faq.answer: Save any files to the outputs/ directory and they will be automatically returned in the response for download.

faq.question: What is the maximum execution time?
faq.answer: You can set timeouts between 1 and 300 seconds. The default is 30 seconds if not specified.

faq.question: Can I use GPU for machine learning tasks?
faq.answer: No, this is a CPU-only environment. For GPU/ML workloads, use dedicated AI image or video generation skills instead.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything locally?
faq.answer: You need the inference.sh CLI tool (infsh) installed and authenticated with your account.
